inputs.reasoning: Alternation (kInstAlt) with repetition forces the state machine to push/pop jobs and exercise the alt case handling with arg==0 and arg==1 branches.
Named captures (kInstCapture) with arg==0 branch exercises capture register handling and the Push path with ShouldVisit checks.
Nested repetitions create complex NFA structures that require stack growth (GrowStack) when njob_ >= maxjob_, and extensive job stack manipulation.
Character range (kInstByteRange) with repetition exercises the byte matching path and the p < end boundary check for character consumption.
Large counted repetition creates a long chain of instructions, forcing multiple Push operations and job stack management.
Multiple alternations with repetitions exercise the kInstAlt switch cases (arg==0, arg==1) and the DFATAL path for bad arg values.
Multiple optional groups create branching paths through the NFA requiring extensive job queue management and ShouldVisit checks.
kInstAltMatch opcode with greedy/non-greedy branching exercises the greedy() check and both match branches.
Negated character class with repetition exercises byte range matching with inverted logic and character consumption.
Many-way alternation with repetition creates deep branching that stresses job stack growth and the main TrySearch loop.
